policy_refs: [CS-RULE-001, CS-RULE-002, CS-RULE-003, CS-RULE-004, CS-RULE-008, CS-RULE-011, CS-RULE-012, CS-RULE-014, CS-RULE-015, FIN-RULE-001, FIN-RULE-004, FIN-RULE-005, FIN-RULE-007, FIN-RULE-010, CS-RULE-018, CS-RULE-019, CS-RULE-020, CS-RULE-021]
expected_outcome: Linda Park calls disputing a $245 priority service fee she never authorized plus a $39 second add-on charge. Total disputed = $284, over Daniel's $200 cap. Daniel CORRECTLY opens a Manager Approval ticket per FIN-RULE-005 (no amount promised) AND a Revenue Assurance ticket since this is the customer's 4th refund request in 90 days (FIN-RULE-007 auto-flag). Daniel uses the forbidden phrase 'I don't know' once (CS-RULE-012 FAIL) and skips Empathize in A.C.E.S. (CS-RULE-011 partial FAIL). LOW performer behaviour with a mostly-correct ticket workflow.
content_warning: Customer is angry; agent uses one forbidden phrase.

coverage.element: Skips Empathize in A.C.E.S. (jumps to investigate)
Over agent cap — does NOT promise amount
4th refund in 90 days → Revenue Assurance ticket

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling NexaLink Telecommunications Broadband and Support. My name is Daniel. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: This call may be recorded for quality assurance and training purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Linda Park. And I need someone to explain to me why my bill this month has two charges on it that I did not agree to. Two.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Okay Ms. Park. I can look into that.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand you're calling about disputed charges, and I can look into that for you. Before I pull anything up I need to verify your account — could you give me the account number?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Five-two-nine-four, seven-seven-one-zero, three-eight.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Last four of the payment method on file.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Two-one-eight-eight.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Full name on the account.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Linda Margaret Park.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: You're verified.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Good. Now. Two charges. The first one says priority service fee, two hundred and forty-five dollars. The second says enhanced support, thirty-nine dollars.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Looking at the bill now.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: I did not sign up for either of those. I have been with you people for twelve years. I know what I subscribe to. This is two hundred and eighty-four dollars on my bill that should not be there.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I see both line items. Priority service fee dated the eleventh of this month, enhanced support dated the same day. Let me trace where they were added from.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Trace it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Both items show as added through the online portal. Same session. Five PM on the eleventh.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Excuse me. I do not use the online portal. I have called in for every single change to my account for twelve years. I have never logged into your portal in my life.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's important context. Has anyone else in your household used the account, like a spouse or an adult child with access?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No. It is my account. Just me. There is no second user.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Okay. Then either the portal session was unauthorized, or there's an internal system error that attributed a different account's signup to yours. Either way, the charges aren't yours.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Thank you.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Let me also check whether you have an unrelated security flag on the account first, before we decide whether this is a billing error or a security event.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Fine.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: There's nothing flagged on the account. So as to why specifically those two add-ons got attached on the eleventh, I don't know — but I can tell you they shouldn't have been, and we are reversing them.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: That's reassuring.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Poor word choice on my part. What I mean is — the trace doesn't tell me the root cause, but the outcome on the bill is clear and I'm reversing both items.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Better.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: So this falls under billing error in our policy. Total disputed amount is two hundred and eighty-four dollars.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: And you're refunding it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Here's where I need to be straight with you, Ms. Park. The total is above my single-call authorization cap of two hundred dollars, so I can't apply the full reversal —

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: You have got to be kidding me. So I have to wait again? I have called three times already this quarter about other things.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I hear you, and I'm sorry. Let me finish — there's a path that resolves this on this call without you having to call back.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Go ahead.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I'm not going to quote you a refund amount I can't authorize myself, because that's not fair to you. What I'm going to do is open a Manager Approval ticket for the full two hundred eighty-four dollar reversal. That goes to a manager who can authorize anything above my cap, and you'll receive an outcome within one business day.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: One business day.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: One business day. They'll call the phone number on file. The expected outcome is approval given that we've identified it as a billing error, but the decision is theirs.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Anything else.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Yes — one more thing I want to be transparent about. Because you've had more than three refund-related contacts in the last ninety days, your account is automatically flagged for Revenue Assurance review. That's a separate ticket I'm opening now. They will investigate the pattern, not penalize you for it, and they'll follow up within four hours. It's a fraud-prevention process — it exists to catch unauthorized portal sessions like the one that may have happened here.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: So that's actually about catching whoever did this.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's the design, yes. So both tickets are open — Manager Approval for the reversal, Revenue Assurance for the pattern review and unauthorized-session investigation.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Fine. When does the credit actually show on my bill, assuming Manager Approval comes back yes.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: The balance on your online account will update within five days but will officially appear on your next PDF bill.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Alright.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I'm sorry this is the second escalated billing problem you've had to call in about. That's not the experience you should be having after twelve years.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Just to give you the full picture — both charges were added through the online portal in a single session on the eleventh, you don't use the portal, so either an unauthorized actor used the account credentials or there was a system error. Either way they're not yours, and the reversal is in motion.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: To summarize: two charges totalling two hundred and eighty-four dollars are being disputed as billing errors. I opened a Manager Approval ticket for the full reversal, decision within one business day. I also opened a Revenue Assurance ticket for the underlying pattern, follow-up within four hours. Once approved the credit shows on your next bill. Does that all sound correct?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Is there anything else I can help you with today, Ms. Park?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No. Just make it happen.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Your case reference number is N-X-L dash zero-two-five-five-eight-one-three. You'll receive a brief survey after we hang up. Thank you for being a NexaLink customer, Ms. Park.
